Other devices are painting as expected, however I got painting issue on this device (GT-I9500). A year ago I faced similar painting issue discussed here: [BoxDecoration has bug when using both Border and BorderRadius](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/86794) I tried to change **UnderlineInputBorder** to **OutlineInputBorder** and got expected results without any issues, because OutlineInputBorder uses `canvas.drawPath()` to draw it. On the other side, UnderlineInputBorder uses `canvas.drawLine() ` which is causing this issue. If I am not wrong **drawLine** calls native code called **Canvas_drawLine**

issues.title: [web, desktop] SelectableText - right click selects a word
comments: The root cause is that the underlying EditableText used by SelectableText still creates a text input connection, and that is interfering with the right click gestures on the framework side since the engine is responding to the right click through updateEditingValue.
3.0 is zions ago. What's the behavior at the current stable?
Hi @FusoraTech the labels are copied from the original issue, but the behavior is still the same on the latest stable/master branch.
I've run your sample in Dartpad, right-click word5, no word selection.

@ishaquehassan thank you for the analysis! I agree with your thoughts regarding the text input connection, that will be tricky to fix since we rely on the connection for keyboard shortcuts on the web. Regarding whether right-clicking to select a word should select the word, on macOS web right-clicking static text does select the word, other platforms may have different behavior.

@ishaquehassan I just checked on native using `NSTextView` and `Text().textSelection(.enabled)` and both had right-click to select word. We should probably be matching this behavior instead of a read only `NSTextField`. While `SelectableText` is implemented as a read-only text field this is an implementation detail and we should not try to match text fields with it, it should instead match static text components on native.

For option B is the override still needed at all? Other platforms other than macOS/iOS do not call select word. Sorry if i'm missing something.

You're right on both points, @Renzo-Olivares. Thanks for checking native.

## Matching `NSTextView` behavior

Agreed. `SelectableText` should behave like a static, selectable text view (`NSTextView`) rather than a read-only text field (`NSTextField`). Right-click to select a word is the correct macOS expectation.

## Is the `onSecondaryTap` override still needed for Option B?

No. Looking at the base class with that target behavior in mind:

| Platform | Base class `onSecondaryTap` | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| macOS / iOS | `selectWord` | correct |
| Windows / Linux / Android / Fuchsia | `selectPosition` | correct (no word selection) |

The `onSecondaryTap` override in `_SelectableTextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilder` is entirely redundant for Option B. The base class already implements the right behavior for every platform.

## Remaining issue on macOS web

The engine sends `TextInput.updateEditingValue` with a word-selection range in response to the right-click, after the framework gesture completes. This overwrites the framework selection. The root cause is `EditableText._shouldCreateInputConnection` returning `true` on web even when `readOnly == true`, which keeps an active text input connection open.

A framework-level fix would be scoping `_shouldCreateInputConnection` to return `false` when `readOnly` is `true` and no input is actually needed. I can update the PR scope to pursue that angle if the team thinks it is a viable path here.

Unfortunately disabling the input connection on the web in the `readOnly` case we still rely on the web text input plugin for keyboard shortcuts even in the `readOnly` case. In this scenario a connection is still needed for the web to properly process the keyboard shortcuts. Flutter delegates the handling of keyboard shortcuts on the web mostly completely to the web text input plugin itself, ignoring them at the framework level. Other reasons it is necessary is the browser context menu, to have a native feeling context menu on the web the framework sends over details about the editable to the text input plugin so it can update it's hidden `TextArea` or `Input` element with the correct context. Solving this is tricky because we rely on the input connection for certain critical editing features but that same connection is causing erroneous changes to the editing state. I thought these mouse events were already being prevented at the engine level https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/758463261fabe1b37f2a4752bfc44a57e10c79b7/engine/src/flutter/lib/web_ui/lib/src/engine/text_editing/text_editing.dart#L1447 cc @mdebbar if you had any thoughts on this issue.

Thanks for digging into this @Renzo-Olivares! 🙏 Makes sense that disabling the connection isn't viable for web given the keyboard shortcuts and context menu dependency.

Looking at `preventDefaultForMouseEvents()` at that engine line, it prevents `mousedown`/`mouseup`/`mousemove` on the hidden element but `contextmenu` isn't in the list. The word selection on web likely sneaks through the browser's native right-click handling on the hidden textarea, which then propagates back as `updateEditingValue`. So the engine-level prevention might not be covering this specific path, which is probably what @mdebbar can shed light on 👀

For #183110, since the framework side is clean and the web issue seems to need engine-level attention, curious what you'd suggest as the best path forward here? 😊

Following up on https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/181833#issuecomment-4034491582, I can confirm that preventing `mousedown/mouseup/mousemove` does not prevent a word from being selected on right click on the web. It seems that selection gesture is being driven by the `contextmenu` event as pointed out by @ishaquehassan. I created a small demo to verify this behavior https://jsfiddle.net/Lw0ym87j/ , in the demo when focusing the textarea you can still right click to select a word even though we are explicitly preventing mouse events.

Here's a solution.

Start by importing "intl" package.
```Dart
import 'package:intl/intl.dart' as intl;
```
and then create this function that will detect if the language is from left or right and return the result as a boolean.
```Dart
bool isRTL(String text) {
  return intl.Bidi.detectRtlDirectionality(text);
}
```

now on your Text() widget, add the above function to the textDirection param to check if the text is left or right and act accordingly.

```Dart
child: Text(
  "Your Text Here",
  textDirection: isRTL("Your Text Here") ? TextDirection.rtl : TextDirection.ltr,
)
```

## Alternative Solution: Content-Based Text Direction Detection

While this issue requests keyboard language detection, I'd like to share an alternative approach that solves the same problem: **content-based text direction detection**.

### Package: flutter_bidi_text

I've published a package that automatically detects and sets text direction based on the actual content being typed, rather than keyboard language:

🔗 https://pub.dev/packages/flutter_bidi_text

### How It Works

Instead of detecting keyboard language, the package uses Flutter's [`intl`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/package-intl_intl/Bidi/estimateDirectionOfText.html) Bidi utilities to analyze the actual characters being entered and determine if they're RTL (Arabic, Hebrew, etc.) or LTR (English, etc.):

```dart
BidiTextField(
  decoration: InputDecoration(hintText: 'Type in any language'),
  onDirectionChanged: (TextDirection? direction) {
    print('Direction changed to: $direction');
  },
)
```

### Why This Approach?

- ✅ **No platform-specific code needed** - Pure Dart using Flutter's intl package utilities
- ✅ **Works on all platforms** - No engine changes required
- ✅ **More reliable** - Based on actual text content, not keyboard settings
- ✅ **Handles mixed content** - Intelligently determines dominant direction
- ✅ **Real-time updates** - Direction changes as users type
- ✅ **Simple drop-in replacement** - You just need to prefix Flutter's built-in text widgets with `Bidi`, e.g `TextField` -> `BidiTextField` and that's it.

### Widgets Available

- `BidiText` - Auto-detecting Text widget
- `BidiRichText` - Auto-detecting RichText with TextSpan support
- `BidiTextField` - Auto-detecting TextField
- `BidiTextFormField` - Auto-detecting TextFormField

### Example

```dart
import 'package:flutter_bidi_text/flutter_bidi_text.dart';

BidiTextField(
  decoration: InputDecoration(
    hintText: 'Type in English or العربية',
    border: OutlineInputBorder(),
  ),
  onDirectionChanged: (direction) {
    print('Direction: ${direction?.name}');
  },
)
```

---

While this doesn't provide direct keyboard language detection, it solves the core use case: **automatically setting TextField direction based on the language being typed**. This content-based approach is often more practical since it works regardless of keyboard settings.

Hope this helps! 🚀

Since `ThemeData.InputDecortationTheme.hintStyle` is available, `hintColor` is on its way out (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/91772), and this change has already made it to `stable`, I would recommend going forward with https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/116346 (or similar) cc @QuncCccccc

@bleroux thanks, but
sorry, i dont understand
so, the color is
onSurfaceVariant
or
hintColor ??= isDark ? Colors.white60 : Colors.black.withOpacity(0.6); 
?

and so better use onSurfaceVariant or InputDecorationTheme? and any side effect?

thanks

> so, the color is `onSurfaceVariant` or `hintColor ??= isDark ? Colors.white60 : Colors.black.withOpacity(0.6);` ?

Currently (Flutter 3.24), the default hint color is:
- `onSurfaceVariant` for Material 3
- `hintColor ??= isDark ? Colors.white60 : Colors.black.withOpacity(0.6);` for Material 2

For Material 3, the default color can be overriden globally using inputDecorationTheme:
```dart
      theme: ThemeData(
        // hintColor: Colors.red, // Material 2 only.
        inputDecorationTheme: const InputDecorationTheme(hintStyle: TextStyle(color: Colors.red)),
      ),
```

issues.title: Critical error on Linux desktop: assertion 'lookup_hash_table(self->pressing_records, physical_key) != 0' failed
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

- Run the code in debug mode on Linux.
- Input, paste, delete some text from the text box. I'm not quite clear what actions triggerred the errors but it's fairly easy to trigger. One sequence that consistently triggers the error is: paste some text using ctrl-V -> delete any character from the end using backspace.

### Expected results

No error.

### Actual results

Tons of errors:

@huycozy Main problem with not having proper `font-size` set in TextField attributes when using the app on the website is that iOS browsers automatically zoom in too small inputs on focus. I don't want to have that input text zoomed in when someone uses my website, but it always happens if I use `ensureSemantics()` in `initState()`

This is important to fix for iOS UX, as the auto zoom behavior isn't always desirable. Web devs can usually prevent this by setting font size greater than 16px, but computed font size in semantic mode is 13.33px regardless of the font size set via TextStyle.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Update the `main.dart` file as follows: 
```Dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  MyHomePage({Key key, this.title}) : super(key: key);
  final String title;

  @override
  _MyHomePageState createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  int _counter = 0;

  void _incrementCounter() {
    setState(() {
      _counter++;
    });
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return FocusableActionDetector(
      shortcuts: <LogicalKeySet, Intent>{
        LogicalKeySet(LogicalKeyboardKey.alt, LogicalKeyboardKey.keyK):
            const CountUpIntent(),
      },
      child: Actions(
        actions: <Type, Action<Intent>>{
          CountUpIntent: CallbackAction<CountUpIntent>(
            onInvoke: (CountUpIntent intent) {
              return setState(
                () {
                  _counter++;
                },
              );
            },
          ),
        },
        child: Scaffold(
          appBar: AppBar(
            title: Text(widget.title),
          ),
          body: Center(
            child: Column(
              mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
              children: <Widget>[
                TextField(),
                Text(
                  'You have pushed the button this many times:',
                ),
                Text(
                  '$_counter',
                  style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headline4,
                ),
              ],
            ),
          ),
          floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton(
            onPressed: _incrementCounter,
            tooltip: 'Increment',
            child: Icon(Icons.add),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class CountUpIntent extends Intent {
  const CountUpIntent();
}

```
3. Run `flutter run -d chrome`
4. Focus the text field
5. Press `Alt + K`
6. Switch to Chinese input using fcitx (Google Pinyin)

10. Switch back to German input
11. Press `Alt + K`

The counter gets incremented with every use of `Alt + K`.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

At step 7: The counter goes up.
At step 8: The text is shown as [tofu](https://www.google.com/get/noto/) for about a second before correctly displaying the Chinese characters. Adding more text displays the characters right away, without tofu.

issues.title: [Web][iOS]: Text field selection with `obscuredText: true` has grey selection
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Use a browser on any iOS device and navigate to https://www.flutterfolio.com or https://app.novospace.com.
2. Type something in the password field.
3. Double-tap the password text to select it.

**Expected results:**
Only the written text should be selected and nothing else.

**Actual results:** 
There is a weird visual grey-highlighted layer in addition to the text selection layer.

Note: This doesn't reproduce on android emulators but I did not test on a real device.

comments: Any updates on this?

This issue is assigned but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!

@a-h-mzd There's no straightforward solution to this because iOS safari doesn't support the pseudoselector used to style selection highlights (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/::selection).

However, we're working on a larger proposal (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120613) which would address this issue.

Hey here,

I've been investigating the https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/124483 issue and would like to add some details regarding the grey selection appearing on iOS web.

1) As @htoor3 mentioned, WebKit on iOS ignores `::selection` CSS pseudo-element.
2) The selection background color appears directly linked to the `caret-color` property. If you set `caret-color: green;`, the selection background becomes a semi-transparent green. If you set `caret-color: transparent;`, the selection background becomes a semi-transparent dark color. It looks like WebKit takes the `caret-color`, adjusts its alpha channel, and uses that color for the selection background. For example, when we set the `caret-color: transparent` (`rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)`), the selection color is set to something like `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.15)`. However, I haven't found any official documentation of this behavior.

You can test this behavior here: https://jsfiddle.net/knevercode/p7zemswq/1/

issues.title: TextInputFormatter breaks dictation on Android
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Focus the TextField
2. Start the dictation feature on the virtual keyboard (I've tested with GBoard and SwiftKey and found the same issue)
3. Say something in the mic

The problem has been reproduced on 3 different android devices, one of them being an Honor 6X BLN-L21 API 24

### Expected results

The dictated text should be displayed in the TextField, with the TextInputFormatter modifications applied. (Here the text should be upper-cased).

### Actual results

Given the dictated sentence: "Hello this should be working"

With GBoard: only the first word is displayed in the TextField, upper-cased: "HELLO".
With SwiftKey: the first word, then the first two words are displayed, upper-cased: "HELLO HELLO THIS".

The rest of the dictated sentence is lost.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) => MaterialApp(
        home: Scaffold(
          body: Center(
            child: TextField(
              inputFormatters: [
                UpperCaseFormatter(),
              ],
            ),
          ),
        ),
      );
}

class UpperCaseFormatter extends TextInputFormatter {
  @override
  TextEditingValue formatEditUpdate(TextEditingValue oldValue, TextEditingValue newValue) =>
      newValue.copyWith(text: newValue.text.toUpperCase());
}
```

</details>

issues.title: [Web][Windows]: RawKeyboard listener not working as intended on web (Ctrl + D opens bookmark)
comments:
Thanks for the report. I believe this is specific to running Flutter web app on Windows platform, that shows the mentioned behavior, because I tried the same on mac and don't see the mentioned behavior. 1. issues.title: TabBarView does not trigger child 'deactivate()' method when swiped if TextField has focus
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

Add `TabBarView `and 3 children to it, one of them with a `TextField` and `autofocus = true`

Change tabs by swiping left/right.

Child `deactivate()` method is not called in the tab with the `TextField`, if it has focus, when swiping away from the tab.

If I call `FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(new FocusNode());` on another tab, then `deactivate()` is called on the tab with the `TextField`.

Changing tab by swiping in not removing the focus from `TextField`.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

`TextField` should loose focus when hidden from view tree when swiping away from its tab.

Child `deactivate()` method should be called the same way it is when changing tabs by tapping on them.


<!-- Finally, paste the output of running `flutter doctor -v` here. -->

```
Doctor summary (to see all details, run flutter doctor -v):
[√] Flutter (Channel master, 2.4.0-5.0.pre.164, on Microsoft Windows [versÃ£o 10.0.19042.1165],
    locale pt-BR)
[√] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 30.0.1)
[√] Chrome - develop for the web
[√] Android Studio (version 4.2)
[√] Connected device (3 available)

• No issues found!

```
comments:
> @dpedrinha
> I tried below code sample based on your details above, for which I see `initState()` getting called upon swipe:
> 
> code sample
> With above code, I do see the textfield keeps focus (because we have explicitly added `autofocus: true` when swiping though.

The example is fine, but I didn't understand your test.

The problem is that `TextField `is keeping the focus even when it's removed from the screen by swiping to a different tab, thus not triggering `deactivate()`. And it keeps the focus until something else gets focused, which only then triggers `deactivate()`.

It should loose focus if the tab is swiped away the same way it does if the tab is changed by tapping on the `TabBar` so the entire tab can be deactivated as it's no longer in the screen.

Try changing tabs a few times by swiping and then by tapping on the `TabBar`. You will notice that `deactivate `is called fewer times when swiping. 

The behavior should be the same by swiping or tapping the `TabBar`.

issues.title: Global text scaling shouldn't be automatically applied on `WidgetSpan`s as scale transforms
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

With the following `TextSpan`, if the ambient text scale factor is `2.0`, the text "INTERRPUTION" will be 2.0x the size of the surrounding text, 
because text widgets in the framework automatically scale `WidgetSpan`s by the ambient text scale factor (2.0x) by applying a 2x scale transform.  See the [Screenshots or Video](#screenshots-or-video) section
 
```dart 
  MediaQuery(
    data: MediaQueryData(textScaleFactor: 2),
    child: Directionality(
      textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
      child: Center(
        child: Text.rich(
          TextSpan(
            children: <InlineSpan>[
              const TextSpan(text: 'a '),
              TextSpan(text: 'pebble'),
              const TextSpan(text: ' in the '),
              WidgetSpan(
                child: SizedBox(
                  width: 40,
                  child: Card(
                    color: Colors.blue,
                    child: RichText(
                      text: const TextSpan(text: 'INTERRUPTION'),
                      textDirection: TextDirection.rtl,
                      textScaleFactor: 2.0,
                    ),
                  ),
                ),
              ),
              const TextSpan(text: 'sky'),
            ],
            style: const TextStyle(),
          ),
          textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
          textScaleFactor: 2,
        ),
      ),
    ),
  ),
```

Semirelated: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/70885

### Expected results

All text in the sample app has a similar size (2x).

### Actual results

The text inside the `WidgetSpan` is way larger (4x).

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
[Paste your code here]
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

![screen](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/31859944/abda027e-5dc1-47f7-8389-f216177cb064)


</details>


### Logs

<details><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
[Paste your logs here]
```

</details>


### Flutter Doctor output

Framework revision dda7d28d25, 2023-05-15 16:47:09 -0400
comments:
What would be the expected behavior here? 

If the thing in the widget span would be an image, we'd expect it to get scaled up by the surrounding text scale factor. Why is text different?

> What would be the expected behavior here?

Specifically in the example, "INTERRUPTION" should be of the same size as the surrounding text, as they have the same `textScaleFactor` and their `TextStyle`s are pretty much the same.

In general I think `WidgetSpan`s shouldn't be scaled automatically (or at least provides a flag to turn the auto-scaling off). An example is if an app has 2 `Chip` widgets with the same exact configuration, with one `Chip` in a text field as an inline widget, and the other in the widget tree as a regular widget. When `textScaleFactor == 1` they should have the exact same appearance. When `textScaleFactor == 2`, both widgets' text is going to be scaled, but if we only scale the padding/border/shadow of the `Chip` inside the text field then the 2 chip widgets are going to look very different. 

> If the thing in the widget span would be an image, we'd expect it to get scaled up by the surrounding text scale factor. 

I think that's a common case we can provide a convenience method/flag/constructor for but such cases aren't easy for WidgetSpan/RenderEditable/RenderParagraph/RichText to detect.

> Why is text different?

Text widgets respond to textScaleFactor themselves (internally they increase the font size). So if there's a nested text widget the text scaling is applied twice, once in the CTM and once in font size.

I am having this problem currently. I want a card around parts of a text, but when I have a `Text` widget nested inside of a `WidgetSpan`, this causes the `textScaleFactor` to be applied twice. And I constantly have a `textScaleFactor` of 2 applied, so it's not just something that only happens when users change their accessibility settings.

Is there a workaround?
I was thinking about wrapping the content with a widget that scales its content by `1/textScaleFactor`, but I don't know of a widget that does this, since `Transform.scale` does not affect the layout size.
Is there a solution short of writing my own `RenderObject`?
It makes sense to introduce a boolean flag to `WidgetSpan` to control the behavior. While inline images should scale with the text by default, in other cases the automatic scaling is undesirable.

@LongCatIsLooong
> While inline images should scale with the text by default [...]

I would not agree with that. Say you have an image in a widget span, but you want to give it rounded corners by wrapping it in a `ClipRRect`. Then the corner radius would also get scaled up, which would likely make it inconsistent with your design.
Also it is called "text scale", which does not imply that anything else is scaled.
So as a developer it would not surprise me if I have to manually scale up non text widgets if they should react to the text scale, since that's the way it is everywhere else.

But there should probably be a `ScaledBox` widget or something, that can scale a child without overflowing.
Currently there is no general way to scale a widget with the font scale, except by using a `WidgetSpan`.
So for a `ScaledBox` with a scale of 2 for example, the constraints passed to the child should be halved and after layouting the child, the `ScaledBox` takes twice the size returned by the child and visually the child is scaled up by a factor of 2.
Then we could do:
```dart
ScaledBox(
  scale: MediaQuery.textScalerOf(context).scale(DefaultTextStyle.of(context).style.fontSize ?? 1),
  child: ...
) 
```
Ideally the `WidgetSpan` would also override the default text style for its child to the same text style a `TextSpan` would have in its place and the widget above could be wrapped in a `TextScaledBox` widget for convenience.

This way you could put the `TextScaledBox` around the image but inside of the `ClipRRect` and everything is perfect :)

I think if you specifically set the text scale factor for the Text in a widgetSpan that are also scaled, it would be expected the text got scale twice.

Can the widget span by default wrap the subtree with MediaQueryData to be noScaling? that way the image will still scale, but the text won't be scaled twice if developer doesn't explicitly pass in another scaler.

> I think if you specifically set the text scale factor for the Text in a widgetSpan that are also scaled, it would be expected the text got scale twice.
> 
> Can the widget span by default wrap the subtree with MediaQueryData to be noScaling? that way the image will still scale, but the text won't be scaled twice if developer doesn't explicitly pass in another scaler.

That sounds reasonable, the only downside I can think of is that every `WidgetSpan` in the app will rebuild in response to any MediaQueryData changes as a result of having to embed a MediaQuery. But if we add a `WidgetSpan.noAutoScale` configurable parameter / constructor that turns off the scale transform we don't need to do the MediaQuery thing right?

> add a WidgetSpan.noAutoScale

If we do this I assume people would need to adjust this depending on what content they are building right? I guess it is probably slightly better for people to manually adjust the scale factor of each text in the widgetspan, but it may still be tricky if the widgetspan child contains both img and text.

> If we do this I assume people would need to adjust this depending on what content they are building right? I guess it is probably slightly better for people to manually adjust the scale factor of each text in the widgetspan, but it may still be tricky if the widgetspan child contains both img and text.

If there's a configurable parameter in WidgetSpan that allows you to turn off the scale transform that is currently automatically applied, for WidgetSpans with both image and text don't you just have to use that parameter to turn off the scale transform?

> image and text don't you just have to use that parameter to turn off the scale transform?

If that is the case, the img will not be scaled but the text will, right? that seems to be weird..
TextScale factor is typically only meant for text. In the regular widget tree it should not scale the image so I don't think it should behave any differently if it gets put in a WidgetSpan?

> the img will not be scaled but the text will, right? that seems to be weird..

This can seem weird at first, but I think it's actually exactly the right behavior, at least by default.

That's because the point of a text-scale factor is that it scales text but not the rest of what's on the screen. Both Android and iOS have two separate settings a user can change to control how big things are:
* One setting scales everything, including text, images, padding, and everything else. Effectively it changes the device pixel ratio.
* The other setting scales only the text, and leaves everything else alone. (Its effect stacks with the scale-everything setting.) This is what's reflected in `_PlatformConfiguration.textScaleFactor` and ultimately in `MediaQuery.textScalerOf`.

The ability to scale only the text is important because it's a way of prioritizing: phone screens are small and layouts often don't have much room to spare. If when the user doubled the text size to make it readable for them, that had the side effect of doubling in size all the padding and icons too, that could leave very little room for the actual text they were trying to read.

In the context of a `WidgetSpan`, this means that any padding, any rounded corners (as mentioned by @Quijx at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/126962#issuecomment-1910715036 above), and images like icons should not be scaled. Any text within it should be scaled, but the `MediaQuery` should handle that already.

Sometimes an image should be scaled like text because it functions as text: for example, an emoji. But when that's the developer's intent, they can apply scaling explicitly (using `MediaQuery.textScalerOf`). They'll have to do that anyway when using such images in any context outside a WidgetSpan.

issues.title: Multiline TextField text selection randomly changes on right click Web
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a multiline TextField
2. Enter some text that goes beyond it's view port and makes it scrollable
3. Do some random scrolling and text selection and right click on web to see the issue

### Expected results

1. When right clicking on a part of text that is not selected or cursor is in another position, the cursor should move to that position
2. When right clicking on a selected text, selection should stay unchanged

### Actual results

1. Cursor moves to another position
2. Selection changes

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const App());
}

class App extends StatelessWidget {
  const App({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) => MaterialApp(
        debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false,
        theme: ThemeData.light(),
        home: HomePage(),
      );
}

class HomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const HomePage({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  _HomePageState createState() => _HomePageState();
}

class _HomePageState extends State<HomePage> {
  final _focusNode = FocusNode();
  final _controller = TextEditingController(
      text:
          'Line1\nLine2\nLine3\nLine4\nLine5\nLine6\nLine7\nLine8\nLine9\nLine10');

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: SizedBox(
        height: 100,
        child: TextField(
          controller: _controller,
          focusNode: _focusNode,
          maxLines: null,
          expands: true,
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

> This is happening because right clicks are handled by the browser (not framework) on web in order to support native context menus. When the user right clicks, the underlying input receives it, and that state is synced with the framework.
> 
> The problem here is that the scroll state between the underlying input and the framework is not synced, so we'll get into a weird state when you scroll down to the further lines. You end up right clicking "Line 3" on the underlying input even though the framework has appeared to scroll that out of view. The solution is to sync the scroll states between the browser and framework.
> 
> @mdebbar and I had a discussion about this as something that would need to be fixed for #120613 - so I'll mark this as a P1.

Update here: this is no longer a pre-req for https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120613, because we'll be delegating pointer event handling responsibilities to the browser.  We had a fix for the OP test case, but it unfortunately breaks down for more complicated multiline values (see: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/135159)
Any update to this?
